title: Meditation as taught by Krishna | BG 8.09 - 8.14 | HG Amitasana Das | HKM Mumbai
speaker: HG Amitasana Dasa

segments.text: So today's discussion is on meditation as taught by Krishna. What meditation? We all
are talking about meditation these days. Lot of people are practicing different kinds of
meditation. What is real meditation? What's objective of that meditation? Translation,
one should meditate upon the Supreme person as the one who knows everything. Now Krishna
in the previous verses saying one should remember me, always think of me. Now it's not an abstract
concept. He is saying remember Him as what? As one who knows everything.
Who is omniscient. As one who is the oldest, who is the controller, everything is moving as per his desire. Who is smaller than the smallest, who is maintainer of everything.
Who is beyond all material conception. Who is inconceivable and who is always a person.
He is luminous like the sun, bright, brilliant, lustrous and being transcendental, he is beyond this material nature.
So it's not an abstract meditation.
One should remember the Lord, one should think of him as what?
He is a transcendental person, he is luminous, he is all-knowing, he is oldest, he is a supreme controller.
Now here in this particular verse, the process of thinking of the Supreme Lord is mentioned.
One cannot meditate on something that is beyond his control.
One cannot meditate on something impersonal or void.
Yes, these days we have meditation, candlelight meditation, you can burn a candle and watch the flame.
Yes, it has some benefit, we can focus our mind, we can concentrate, it improves our concentration, that's all great.
But meditation in true sense is meditating on?
We link ourselves with the Supreme Lord.
Now word achintya is used in this verse.
The Lord cannot be conceived by our blunt material senses.
He is transcendental, he is adhokshaja.
He is a supreme person, but the process to see him is not through our blunt material eyes.
Therefore, we need the help of the Vedas, our scriptures.
To know more about this person.
We are too small and insignificant compared to this gigantic creation.
Have you heard of this Hubble telescope?
So with this Hubble telescope, they are able to watch galaxies.
Milky Way, we are part of Milky Way, this solar system, the sun, family of planets and many more stars, each of them as big as sun.
We are part of a galaxy called Milky Way.
And they say there are millions of such galaxies in this universe.
And you can see these galaxies through these telescopes.
Now, just see this planet Earth.
How gigantic it has, huge continents.
If you go by a Boeing flying at 900 miles per hour, you know it takes 18 hours, 20 hours, 25 hours.
Go from one side to other side of the planet.
Plane is flying at 900 miles, it takes 8 hours to cross that ocean.
Now like this we have other planets and this whole planet we have a solar system.
And there are millions of stars, each of them as big as sun.
Each of them having its own family of planets.
And imagine in that planet Earth.
We are sitting in a small city, relatively speaking.
And in that city, a small place, Powai.
And in that Powai, you know, a small school.
And in that school, in the fifth floor, in an auditorium.
How small, insignificant we are compared to this Earth.
And this Earth when compared to this galaxy and this universe.
Now, this Lord, He is the creator of millions of such universes.
Each of them having millions of galaxies.
Each of them having so many stars.
In fact, through the Hubble telescope, they figured out that these galaxies are moving away.
They are not stationary in one place.
And they are receding at a certain speed.
They figured out that if all these galaxies are receding.
And they are constantly going away.
Then at one point of time, they all should have originated from a particular point.
And if you know the speed at which they are receding.
And you work backward that you can calculate.
There was a point of time when all these galaxies were exploded from a point.
They said there was a point.
There is a person who has written.
A scientist who has written this article.
Who says there was a point.
Which had sum total of all the energies.
And this whole thing started expanding.
And he being a believer of God says.
Who is beyond our sense perception.
Many people conclude he does not exist.
Because we see people who are limited by time and space.
I can very confidently say you have not gone to Mayapur.
Because you cannot be in two places simultaneously.
Whereas we know that Lord is all present.
So they use that logic and says.
How can the Lord who is all present.
Be a person who is limited by time and space.
People are limited by time and space.
But we cannot use material logic.
Lord is not like a material person like us.
Every person whom we come across.
They are subjected to old age.
And we understand the Lord is.
Is not subjected to all this.
Using material logic we come to a wrong conclusion.
So therefore if we have to understand about that Lord.
We have to follow a different process.
Take help of descending process of acquiring knowledge.
There are two ways of acquiring knowledge.
By gripping more and more data.
That's what the science is doing.
At one point of time we believed that earth is flat.
We further did lot of research.
Atom is not the smallest particles.
So we are building our body of knowledge through observation.
We are going upwards in our search of absolute truth.
If we have to understand about the Lord.
Which process we have to use?
A small ant in this room.
Some ant hole in this hall.
You should be up to date about what's happening in the world.
Can it ever know? Think about it. It also has a world view. When it moves around, it also has an understanding of this world, figuring out what's going on.
Sunday comes, 4 o'clock, what do we do here? It has a world view.
On its own, using ascending process, it can never figure out what's happening in other parts of the world.
But it can figure out using the descending process.
You can tell Lalul-e-Adab ko jail ho gaya.
One who at the time of death fixes his life-wear between the eyebrows and in full devotion.
Mind you, this is something which all of us will have to go through.
Now somebody says, Swamiji, aap death ke baarein bahut bolte ho.
Yes, we can always set aside, close our eyes.
In fact, there is a time when the rabbit is being chased by the lion.
It runs, runs, runs, runs and finally at one point of it realizes the tiger is running very fast and he will not be able to escape.
So at one point it just gives up, sits in one place, closes the eyes and thinks.
So, we all have to face this moment of death.
And we have faced it many, many times.
Now in human form of life, we can face differently.
Prabhupada says one is a Kripana, a miserly man.
If he dies like a cat and a dog.
What is the meaning of dying like a cat and a dog?
Born in ignorance, died in ignorance.
In between did lot of gymnastics.
He is a miserly man who got this human form of life.
Had an opportunity to realize the self but without perfecting his life, died in ignorance.
One who at the time of death fixes his life between the eyebrows and in full devotion engages himself in remembering the Supreme Lord will certainly attain to the Supreme Personality of Godhead.
So Krishna in the Gita talks about
Beyond this material world, there is spiritual world.
This world which we are living in is vyakta.
It has come into being and will one day get annihilated.
But Krishna says parastasmatu bhavanyo beyond this material creation there is a spiritual creation.
Which is beyond vyakta-avyakta-sanatana and it is sanatana.
And here Krishna is saying that one who quits his body remembering me.
In full concentration with the devotion will certainly attain to the Supreme Personality of Godhead.
Lot of people ask moksha, mukti.
You know we all have a weird concept.
Moksha means all our hands will go away and suddenly we will start flying in the air.
There is a material world where the soul is transmigrating life after life.
And we come out of this transmigration.
At the time of death the mind must be fixed in devotion on the Supreme Godhead.
If the mind is not fixed what happens?
Whatever we are thinking, whatever our consciousness is, that state we attain in next life.
Did we all choose our birth?
We will be born in Pavai.
We will be born in this family.
We will take birth as Indian.
Some atma is born as let's say a cat.
Many of you may have pets.
You throw a piece of stone it immediately responds.
There is an experiencer like you and me.
That atma and the atma in me is not different.
So, based on our thinking, we get the next body.
The mind must be fixed in devotion.
It's not just for time pass, it's not for peace of mind, it is not for relaxation, it is not for, you know, it gives us a good feeling, it's not for all that mundane thing.
Human life, there is a higher purpose. Higher purpose is what? To purify our existence.
So that we can not die like cats and dogs.
Die with remembrance of the Lord. If you have to die with remembrance of the Lord, you have to practice remembering the Lord.
And to practice remembering the Lord, you have to spend time. Any practice takes time.
Saini and Eval practices badminton. How many hours in a day, anyone guess?
It doesn't come cheaply. The cricketers, I am sure they must be putting lot of time practicing.
Even though they may be very accomplished, very this thing, still they may be practicing to improve their skills.
Pure devotee is the one who is always thinking of Krishna, sees everything as manifestation of Krishna and His energies.

Pure devotee can live anywhere and create the atmosphere of Vrindavan, so one thing is yes, Vrindavan which is a spiritual place but if we engage in devotional service, the place where we are staying can also be transformed into Vrindavan.
Vrindavan is a place where the Lord is the centre.
So even our house can be Vrindavan.
If the centre thing which is happening in the house is the Lord, His devotion, His worship, His glorification, His chanting, that is the main thing happening, that house is Vrindavan.
A pure devotee cannot forget the Supreme Lord for a moment and similarly the Supreme Lord cannot forget His pure devotee for a moment and this is a reciprocal relationship.
It is not that we keep doing devotion, devotion, devotion.
A lot of people sometimes says, you know, when people get into problems, difficulties, some people have some ailments, some diseases, incurable, then we tell them, you know, worship God.

And then they actually worship God and say, nothing is happening, I have done a lot of Pooja, I have done a lot of Paath, I have gone here, I have gone there, it was 7.30 pm, I have done a Pooja of Shani, it is not happening.
Because we are disconnected from the Lord.
At that point when we need help of the Lord, we are seeking, give me help, give me help.
When actually we were in good shape, you know, we were completely forgetful of the Lord.
So when we remember the Lord, when we have invested time in the Lord, the Lord is reciprocating.
If we are dear to the Lord, Lord is dear to us.
As you surrender, so shall I reciprocate.
The ball is in our court, how much we want.
Any questions? And we move on. Yes.
How do we? How do we do Om chanting?
We strongly recommend chanting of Hare Krishna Mantra, which is also called Maha Mantra.
And is specifically recommended for this age of Kali.
Again, it is not our opinion.
There is an Upanishad called Kali Santrana Upanishad.
In that Upanishad, it is mentioned, Hare Krishna, Hare Krishna, Krishna Krishna, Hare Krishna.
The 16 names is there, full mantra is there.
Iti Shodashaka Naam Naam. Shodashaka means 16.
Naata Parotra Upaya. You will not find a Parotra.
Parotra means higher, superior, better, more effective.
If you search scrutinizingly all the Vedas and try to figure out what is the Upaya, what is the solution, what I should be doing,
you will not find a better Upaya.
This is from the Kali Santrana Upanishad.
specifically for this age, the Acharyas, knowing this age,
has recommended chanting of this Mantra, which is also called Mahamantra.
So if you are interested in chanting,
Om, yes we can chant, but it would be better, it is advisable, it is recommended that we chant this Mahamantra.
So there is nothing wrong per se.
If suppose somebody is chanting Om, I am not saying that you are, it is wrong.
So it is like, I was telling about three aspects of absolute truth.
They all are referring to the same, just like sunshine,
When sunshine comes to your room, you say sun has come.
When sun comes, everything will end.
Because sunshine is sun, in one sense.
Likewise Brahman is an aspect of Bhagwan.
And Om chanting is a representation of the impersonal aspect of the Lord.
Whereas Hare Krishna Mahamantra is representing the Lord.
Representing the Bhagwan aspect of the Supreme Lord.
Krishna, Rama, Hare refers to the energy of the Lord.
So Om is representation of the impersonal aspect of the Lord.
Whereas Hare Krishna Mahamantra is directly referring to the Supreme Person.
So it is specifically recommended for this age.

I will be on his side.

of burning and fire and smoke and torture and anguish

He's all-powerful, all-present, all-knowing, and all-wise.

They pay no taxes, and somehow they always need money.
I'll be on the side of this person.
And all of us are in agreement about what he said?
How many of us are in agreement, by the way?
So two, three points which come up in his specific short talk.
He says that there's an invisible man up in the sky who is witnessing everything, everyone.
And then, he has a list of 10 things.
If you do any of these things, at the end of the life, you are pushed into a hell.
By the way, he's a kind, loving father.
So how do we counter this?
He's an invisible man who is up in the sky, who is witnessing all what we are doing.
Is it true, yes or no?
How is it possible for him to witness?
Using mundane material logic, we come to understand how can there be a person like that?
Who is all-knowing, who is witnessing everything.
But we saw in this verse, what did we see?
And the next point, what he says, that there is a hell where a person who disobeys certain conduct or does some sin, he's punished, he suffers, but still he is all-loving.
How do we reconcile with these two things?
We see in this world, a lot of people are suffering.
If God is all-good, all-kind, all-loving, then why are they suffering?
Just like even in this state, the government is for the people, by the people. Am I right?
The government is for the people, by the people. Am I right?
The government is for the people, by the people. Am I right?
The government wants the election manifesto.
We'll serve you in such a way.
But the government also is forced to make the prison house.
Just like what happened to Lalu Yadav.
Because there are some people who will violate the laws.
And to maintain law and order,
So, likewise, the Lord has made the laws.
And those who violate the laws,
He doesn't want us to suffer.
But if we choose, using our free will,
to do something which is not in our self-interest,
Every action has an equal and opposite reaction.
So we cannot blame the Lord that He makes us suffer and puts in the hell.
If we act in a way, just like a murderer,
Now you cannot blame the government.
You cannot blame the Supreme Court judge.
My God, He is so, so cruel.
He has put those people behind bars.
Because they have acted like that.
But why does God need money?
You know, we say that He is all-powerful.
But why does God need money?

it is not that He wants that.
